Optimize PHR BA: vectorized residuals + sparse Jacobian (100x speedup)

Root cause of slowness:
- Old code used Python for-loops inside _phr_residuals (called ~500+ times by LM)
- method='lm' (dense Jacobian) required n_vars extra FD evaluations per step
- scipy Rotation object creation per call was expensive

Fixes applied:
- Fully vectorized _phr_residuals_vec using NumPy einsum (no Python loops)
- Batch Rodrigues formula _rotvec_to_matrix_batch: (N,3)->>(N,3,3) via algebra
- Sparse Jacobian pattern _build_sparsity: each obs depends only on its cam+pt
- Switched least_squares from method='lm' to method='trf' + jac_sparsity
- Reduced max_nfev from 500 to 200 (warm start needs fewer iterations)

Results (BA time, same rotation accuracy):
- llff_fern:  42.9s -> 0.06s  (700x faster)
- kitchen:    26.2s -> 0.04s  (650x faster)
- llff_flower:32.0s -> 0.06s  (530x faster)
- room:        0.7s -> 0.02s
- sceaux:     38.5s -> 0.02s (1900x faster)

Total PHR time now 0.18-0.55s (was 17-43s), competitive with FF and SfM.
Rotation accuracy unchanged (PHR still best on all scenes with GT).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

re-extraction for any container image whose cached vulnerabilities
didn't come from a cosign attestation, ensuring the migration from

when image digests haven't changed.

fix: wait for config commit + drain delay before test ends

Two fixes for TDC smoke test functional failures:

1. Driver now calls wait_cfg_accept() AFTER program_global_ctrl() to
   ensure the sequencer has committed the requested config to the
   active image before injecting events. Without this, events arrive
   while active_global_enable is still 0.

2. EOT handler now waits drain_timeout_ns (default 50us) before
   returning, giving the MPTDC pipeline time to produce and serialize
   packets before the monitors are killed by env.run() join_any exit.

commit.message:
fix: prevent index.db bloat with auto_vacuum and startup maintenance (#378)

Enable SQLite incremental auto_vacuum so freed pages are reused for new
writes instead of accumulating on the freelist permanently. Add a
maintenance() method that runs FTS optimize, incremental_vacuum, WAL
checkpoint, and ANALYZE — time-gated to fire only on the first indexing
pass after startup (4-hour throttle), making it a complete no-op during
the 5-second auto-refresh cycles.

Root causes addressed:
- auto_vacuum=NONE caused 200 MB of permanent freelist waste (35% of DB)
- FTS5 segments never optimized during incremental indexing
- WAL never explicitly checkpointed after heavy writes

Changes:
- index_db/mod.rs: ensure_incremental_auto_vacuum() with one-time VACUUM
  migration, maintenance()/maintenance_force()/run_full_maintenance()
  with epoch-based throttling, Migration 10 for maintenance_state table
- lib.rs: reindex_search_content calls time-gated maintenance(),
  rebuild_search_content calls maintenance_force() (bypass throttle)
- 147 tests pass, zero warnings

fix: keep csr_if.rsp_ready=1 to prevent decoder ST_HOLD_RSP deadlock

The CSR decoder FSM goes ST_IDLE → ST_HOLD_RSP on every write,
asserting rsp_valid and waiting for rsp_ready to return to IDLE.
The BFM's csr_write task never drove rsp_ready (init 0), so the
decoder got stuck after the very first write — req_ready stayed
low and all subsequent transactions hung.

Fix: initialise rsp_ready=1 (always high) so the decoder can
always retire responses immediately. Removed the toggle logic
in csr_read since rsp_ready no longer needs gating.

commit.message:
refactor: reorganize repo into /web /iOS /Android structure

- Move iosApp → iOS/Xcode (iOS project isolated)
- Create web/ directory with deployment files:
  - Dockerfile (multi-stage KMP build)
  - nginx.conf (SPA routing, WASM MIME types)
  - .github/workflows/deploy-web.yml (CI/CD)
  - test-build.sh/bat (local testing)

- Create Android/ with build documentation
- Keep composeApp/ at root (shared KMP module for all targets)
- Update root README.md with clear structure docs
- Add platform-specific READMEs:
  - web/README.md
  - iOS/README.md
  - Android/README.md

Structure is now:
- composeApp/ → shared KMP Compose code (commonMain, androidMain, iosMain)
- web/ → deployment automation (Docker, Nginx, GitHub Actions)
- iOS/Xcode/ → iOS project
- Android/ → Android docs

Easier to find platform-specific setup while keeping shared code centralized.
